2606.27786.title: SHIFT: Gate-Modulated Activation Steering for Knowledge Conflict Mitigation in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
2606.27786.paper_summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) enhances LLMs by incorporating external knowledge to support response generation. However, conflicts between retrieved context and parametric knowledge have emerged as a critical challenge in RAG systems. To mitigate such conflicts, numerous studies have attempted to identify and edit knowledge-related internal neurons, aiming to improve the ability of LLMs to rely on contextual evidence during generation. However, these neuron-level approaches may introduce unintended cascading effects that compromise the general capabilities of LLMs, as the modified neurons are often entangled with broader model behaviors and functionalities. In this paper, we introduce SHIFT, a novel framework that reformulates neuron-level modification as learnable gate modulation, allowing LLMs to adaptively regulate internal activations for knowledge conflict resolution. Technically, our SHIFT equips LLMs with a lightweight gate module and optimizes fewer than 0.01% trainable parameters while keeping the backbone model frozen. During generation, the gate module adjusts the model's internal representations to adaptively leverage contextual and parametric knowledge. Extensive experiments on six datasets validate the effectiveness of our SHIFT in comparison with various competing baselines. All datasets and code are available at https://github.com/OpenBMB/SHIFT.
2606.27786.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）透過整合外部知識來增強大型語言模型（LLMs）的回應生成能力。然而，檢索到的上下文與參數知識之間的衝突已成為RAG系統中的一個關鍵挑戰。為了減輕這些衝突，許多研究嘗試識別和編輯與知識相關的內部神經元，旨在提高LLMs在生成過程中依賴上下文證據的能力。然而，這些神經元層級的方法可能會引入意想不到的連鎖效應，從而損害LLMs的整體能力，因為被修改的神經元通常與更廣泛的模型行為和功能相互糾纏。在本文中，我們介紹了SHIFT，一個新穎的框架，將神經元層級的修改重新定義為可學習的閘控調節，允許LLMs自適應地調節內部激活以解決知識衝突。在技術上，我們的SHIFT為LLMs配備了一個輕量級的閘控模塊，並優化了少於0.01%的可訓練參數，同時保持主幹模型不變。在生成過程中，閘控模塊調整模型的內部表示，以自適應地利用上下文和參數知識。在六個數據集上的廣泛實驗驗證了我們的SHIFT相較於各種競爭基準的有效性。所有數據集和代碼均可在 https://github.com/OpenBMB/SHIFT 獲得。
2606.27786.author: Ruochang Li et.al.
2606.27786.authors: Ruochang Li, Pengcheng Huang, Zhenghao Liu, Yukun Yan, Huiyuan Xie, Yu Gu, Ge Yu, Maosong Sun
